parsed.artifact_title: Preconditions for Comparing a Multi-Wake Continuation with Append-Only Output

claims.text: The second wake must consume state produced by the first wake rather than reconstructing it from the full corpus.
Open items are closed by the framework-bound continuation, not by an append-only shortcut.
The model does not choose the terminal surface for the continuation wake.

parsed.conclusion: Before a multi-wake continuation can be meaningfully compared with append-only output, it must demonstrate three properties: (1) the second wake consumes state carried forward from the first wake rather than recomputing it from the full corpus; (2) any open items are resolved solely by the framework-bound continuation, never by an append-only shortcut; and (3) the model does not select which surface serves as the terminal surface for the continuation wake. Absent any one of these properties, the comparison would be invalid because the multi-wake continuation would not respect the append-only constraint under which the baseline was produced.
declared_losses: No empirical evidence is provided showing that any concrete multi-wake continuation satisfies all three required properties; the note states necessary conditions but does not demonstrate sufficiency or realizability.
parsed.recommended_action: Require a multi-wake continuation to present affirmative evidence that all three conditions hold before accepting it as a valid comparator for append-only baselines.
